12.reason: Error: The condition does not check if characters are of the same type (lowercase, uppercase, digit) or if s[i-1] < s[i+1]; it also doesn't handle cases where expansion should not occur (e.g., B-e), leading to incorrect expansion.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The condition does not check if characters are of the same type (lowercase, uppercase, digit) or if s[i-1] < s[i+1]; it also doesn't handle cases where expansion should not occur (e.g., B-e), leading to incorrect expansion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This loop does not check if the characters around '-' are of the same type and in ascending order, nor does it handle cases where expansion should not occur (e.g., B-e). It also does not validate that s[i-1] and s[i+1] are within valid ranges [a-z], [A-Z], [0-9].
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  This loop should only execute if expansion is valid; the condition s[i+1]-s[i-1]-1 can be negative or zero, and it does not consider type mismatch (e.g., 'B' and 'e' produce a positive count but should not be expanded).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Assumes s[i+1] > s[i-1] and same type; no handling for invalid cases (e.g., left>=right, different types). Also may access out-of-bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing validation: must check if s[i-1] and s[i+1] are of the same type and s[i-1] < s[i+1] before expanding, otherwise invalid cases like 'B-e' are incorrectly expanded.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Unconditionally performs expansion without type checking, causing mismatched types like 'B-e' to be incorrectly expanded.

14.reason: Error: When j=0, m[j-1] accesses m[-1], which is out-of-bounds and undefined behavior. Also, this logic assumes m[j-1] is the previous character, but it should start from s[i-1]+1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: When j=0, m[j-1] accesses m[-1], which is out-of-bounds and undefined behavior. Also, this logic assumes m[j-1] is the previous character, but it should start from s[i-1]+1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This incorrectly uses m[j-1] which may be uninitialized or from a previous character, instead of starting from s[i-1] and incrementing properly. Also, it does not output the first character of the expansion (s[i-1]).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  This line assumes m[j-1] exists and is the correct starting character, but it does not handle invalid expansions (where the '-' should be output) and relies on the previous character being set correctly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This assumes the previous character in m is the left bound of the range, but it may not be if '-' appears at the start or after non-expanded content. Also, no validation is done to check if both sides of '-' are same type (digit/upper/lower) and valid for expansion.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line assumes expansion is always valid and uses m[j-1], which may not be the correct previous character (e.g., when '-' is at start or after non-expanded chars). Also, it doesn't check if both sides are same type or if s[i+1] > s[i-1].]
